content.method: Pilot study introducing a coaching-resistant exam/interview format in a subset of gimnáziums, tracking applicant-pool SES composition pre/post, paired with qualitative interviews of families on adaptive strategies; compare against natural experiments in other countries that redesigned selection instruments.
content.question: Would SES-weighted or coaching-resistant admissions redesign (T1) meaningfully change enrollment composition, or would elite families adapt through alternative investment channels (e.g., targeted coaching for new criteria, private tutoring pivot) to fully offset intended gains?
content.why_it_matters: T1 is the least structurally disruptive proposal and thus politically attractive, but its value depends entirely on whether family-capital adaptation neutralizes the reform, a dynamic already observed internationally (Finland's emphasized classes) but untested specifically for Hungarian admissions redesign.
